texts.full_text: you understand the meaning of your existence and why you are a special individual. Meditation is extremely personal, and is different for every person that practices it. One thing is clear though, people that practice meditation are more focused, alert and tranquil. lesson three: types of meditation As there are many different personalities, there are also different types of meditation. In this chapter, you will learn what those methods of meditation are as well as the benefits of each. Learning about the different methods of meditation will allow you to select the method that will best work for you. Every individual has different preferences and knowing the various approaches to meditation will greatly benefit you. When learning the different types of meditation it is important that you keep an open mind. Be sure to take the time to learn the intricacies of each method. If you do not find a method that works for you initially, you will know that there are other methods available for you to try. lesson four: what happens during meditation You have heard of the benefits of meditation, but what actually happens while you are How and why does meditation work and why does having this information benefit you? In this chapter you will learn the mechanics behind meditation. Starting on the path of meditation is also a big step. Meditation changes how you view life and how you live your life. How will meditation you? Why will it affect you? You have heard the stress can affect your health. You have also heard that meditation can help you relieve stress. texts.full_text:
check online documents function checkOnlineDocs($array, &$onlineDocs) { foreach($array as $nr => $name) { $xml = simplexml_load_file("http://www.example.com/docs.php?live_id=".$nr); if($xml->liveDocs->download != 0) { $onlineDocs[$nr] = $name; } } } // Check online documents from both arrays checkOnlineDocs($externdoc, $onlineDocs); checkOnlineDocs($anotherArray, $onlineDocs); // Echo online documents foreach($onlineDocs as $nr => $name) { echo 'Download: ' . $name . ' (ID: ' . $nr . ')' . '
'; } ?> ``` In this script, I've created a new array `$anotherArray` and a function `checkOnlineDocs` that takes an array and a reference to `$onlineDocs` array. The function checks for online documents and adds them to the `$onlineDocs` array.

texts.full_text: . The regiment was always mounted on black horses. At the end of 1755, a company of light dragoons was added to the regiments. These light dragoons had brass helmets. During the Seven Years' War, the regiment was under the command of: in 1758: colonel Bland Service during the War In the summer of 1758, the regiment was among the first British contingent (6,000 men) sent to reinforce the Allied army of Ferdinand of Brunswick in Germany. The contingent embarked at Gravesend on July 19, disembarked at Emden on August 3 1758 and arrived at Coesfeld on August 17, after marching through a very heavy rain. In June 1759, the regiment was part of the main Allied army under the command of the duke Ferdinand of Brunswick. On August 1, the regiment was present at the battle of Minden where it was deployed in the first line of the right hand column under lord George Sackville. This cavalry corps did not take part in the battle despite several orders requesting its intervention. Lord Sackville was later court-martialed and lost his command. On July 10 1760, the regiment was with the Hereditary Prince at the combat of Corbach. After the defeat, the rear-guard was so hard pressed that the prince only extricated it by putting himself at the head of two squadrons of the 1st and 3rd Dragoon Guards, and leading them to a despera

texts.full_text: violation of Code § 18.2-479. We hold that the defendant was not in custody, and therefore, could not be convicted of escape. Accordingly, we reverse the judgment of conviction and dismiss the indictment. The evidence at trial established these facts: The appellant had been arrested for driving while intoxicated on September 18, 1992, when he was involved in a traffic accident with another vehicle as it exited I-495 onto Route #7 (Fairfax County). After being transported to Inova Fairfax Hospital by ambulance and receiving treatment there from approximately midnight until about 3:00 or 3:30 A.M., the appellant refused further medical attention, became disruptive in his behavior toward hospital personnel when they attempted to draw blood for a toxicology test (which he had consented to at time of arrest), was combative with police officers who responded and were summoned by security guards called from other parts of the building because of this activity. The officer testified that appellant "was very abusive, calling us names," but admitted on cross-examination there was no physical contact made between himself or his partner (who also had been present at time) nor any threat thereof toward either police officers *18 or hospital personnel; rather he described the situation as one in which they were trying to calm him down and get blood drawn. At some point, two additional deputies arrived on scene with a restraint chair from another part of Inova Fairfax Hospital where it was used for combative patients who could not be controlled otherwise due its design - padded arm rests/straps across chest & legs; ankle restraints attached behind back rest via metal pole running down e
